ID:       ddx-3c154349
Title:    ddx work: auto-triage on review BLOCK and other recoverable failure modes
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 1
Labels:   phase:2, story:10, area:agent, kind:fix, observed-failure
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-e34994e2
Desc:     Currently when a bead hits a post-merge review BLOCK, the drain loop emits the BLOCK event, leaves the bead open, and moves to the next ready bead. The BLOCK then sits in the queue waiting for a human. Observed in this drain: ddx-a9edd569 (S3_B label-aware collision) hit BLOCK and was abandoned with no triage.

The loop should take an automated triage action when recoverable failure modes are observed, rather than dropping. Failure modes worth triaging:
- post-merge review BLOCK → either (a) re-attempt with the BLOCK reasons embedded in the next prompt's context, or (b) escalate to next tier in the S10_2 ladder, or (c) file a follow-up bead capturing the BLOCK reasons + mark the original needs_human
- 'staging tracker: Unable to create .git/index.lock' (git contention — see sibling bead) → retry with backoff after the lock clears
- 'execution_failed' from substantive (non-infrastructure) error → escalate per S10 ladder
- 'no_changes' (agent decided nothing to do) → re-evaluate against the bead AC; if still seems valid, file a clarification follow-up rather than mark already_satisfied

Triage policy is a config-driven, per-failure-mode mapping. Default policy: escalate-then-needs-human after N attempts at the next tier.
Accept:   1. Loop has a triage step after each attempt's terminal event. 2. Per-failure-mode triage policy configurable via .ddx/config.yaml or flag. 3. Default policy: BLOCK→re-attempt-with-context, then escalate-tier on second BLOCK, then needs_human on third. 4. Lock contention errors retry with backoff (see sibling bead for the lock fix itself). 5. Tests cover at least: BLOCK→re-attempt, BLOCK→escalate, lock-error→retry. 6. Existing review-pairing-degraded events surface in the triage decision (so re-attempt gets the right reviewer pairing context).

input.content:
No source changes in this attempt. The parent bead ddx-3c154349 was decomposed into three children (recorded in the bead notes) and the implementation lives in those children, not in the parent. The previous attempt's review BLOCK on this same bead correctly observed that "Diff contains only execution manifest/result metadata for the parent bead. No source changes implementing triage step, config policy, BLOCK re-attempt, lock retry, or tests."

What is done:
- Decomposition already filed:
  * 0a700bd2 — Triage policy framework: config schema + decision engine
  * 5f67936e — Triage integration: review BLOCK / REQUEST_CHANGES wiring (depends on framework)
  * 2b64cb0a — Triage integration: lock retry-with-backoff + execution_failed/no_changes (depends on framework)
- Dependency edges already recorded; children sit in `open` state in the queue.

What is blocking:
- Nothing concrete to implement at the parent level. AC items 1–6 are each owned by one of the three children. Re-attempting the parent monolithically (~triage engine + config + BLOCK wiring + lock retry + tests + pairing-degraded surfacing) violates the size-check guidance in the execute-bead instructions: it spans unrelated subsystems and would exceed the bounded-context budget that produced the original decomposition.

What a follow-up attempt would need:
- The parent should not be re-attempted. It should either (a) be closed as decomposed-only once all three children land, or (b) be marked needs_human so the orchestrator stops re-queuing it. The drain loop should treat parent-of-decomposed beads as already-satisfied when their children cover the AC.

This rationale exists so the post-merge reviewer sees evidence of intentional no_changes rather than a harness failure.

data.text: Wrote no_changes_rationale.txt. The parent bead ddx-3c154349 was already decomposed into three children (0a700bd2, 5f67936e, 2b64cb0a) — implementation lives there, not in the parent. Re-attempting the full AC monolithically would violate the size-check guidance. Exiting without a commit; rationale documents the decomposition for the reviewer.
